lead_headline: Restaurant sector posts second straight net-positive week with 23 openings vs. 33 closures—down from 55 closures last week—while foreclosure filings surge 145% to 49 cases totaling $22.2M.

restaurant.blurb: 23 openings, 33 closures over the past seven days—still net negative, but a marked improvement from last week's 32-to-55 bloodletting. The closure rate dropped 40% week-over-week. Notable debuts: Mendocino Farms landed at Union Station, Wapos Cantina opened in Englewood's Rosedale, and The Agora brought a new bar-and-grill to Sunnyside. Over 30 days, the sector remains down 29 net locations.

foreclosure.blurb: 49 new filings dropped this week—up 145% from last week's 20—with total exposure of $22.2M. Leading the pack: an $8.1M filing at 7380 S. Clinton in Arapahoe County. Weld County holds the highest active caseload at 305, followed by Larimer's 262. 45 auctions teed up for the next seven days. Filing velocity is accelerating after last week's lull.

reputation.blurb: 18 mentions across eight sources this week—up from zero last week, marking the tool's inaugural tracking period. The Denver Post led citations with seven pickups, followed by Hoodline (3) and Instagram (3). Notable hits: Denverite covered the Brown Palace sale opportunity, while Reddit's r/Denver and 9News also referenced BusinessDen reporting. Google News RSS drove 17 of 18 discoveries.
